notes: SirTM ADP-ribosylates histone H2B in human colonocytes. Direct in vitro biochemistry: purified SirTM + recombinant H2B + NAD+ produced a modification detected by anti-ADP-ribose Western blot (Chang et al. 2026, Figure 5G). Substrate identity confirmed by mass spectrometry of immunoprecipitated ADP-ribosylated proteins from P. micra-infected colonocytes (Figure 5E). H2BC4 (P62807) used as canonical representative paralog; the pan-H2B antibody used in vivo (Proteintech 15857-1-AP) means the exact paralog modified in vivo is ambiguous. Chang et al. also demonstrated that SirTM lacks deacetylase activity despite SIR2-family domain prediction (Figure 5C), confirming ADP-ribosyltransferase as the sole enzymatic function.

Recombinant GST-FBA pulled down ITGA6 from HT29 colorectal cancer cell membrane fractions; reciprocal co-immunoprecipitation with anti-ITGA6 antibody confirmed the interaction in HT29, CACO-2, HCT116, and NCM460 cells (Huang et al. 2024, Cell Host Microbe, Figure 2). FBA is a surface-displayed moonlighting glycolytic enzyme (fructose-1,6-bisphosphate aldolase class II) that acts as the primary adhesin of P. stomatis on the colonocyte surface.

Same GST-FBA pull-down and reciprocal anti-ITGB4 IP evidence as for ITGA6, from the same Huang et al. 2024 experiment (Figure 2). ITGA6 and ITGB4 form the alpha6beta4 integrin heterodimer; FBA binding to this heterodimer activates downstream ERBB2-MEK-ERK-p90RSK signaling and drives CRC progression and RTK-inhibitor resistance.
